summary: Gruber posts a periodic self-promotional piece about the three ways to sponsor Daring Fireball: weekly RSS feed sponsorships, display ads (his homegrown replacement for The Deck), and sponsoring The Talk Show podcast. He acknowledges the tension between disliking self-promotion and needing to sell ads to sustain the site. He highlights the weekly sponsorship model's success since 2007, noting that repeat sponsors validate the format's effectiveness. The post also reveals a last-minute cancellation creating a discounted opening.
key_points: Weekly RSS feed sponsorships have been DF's top revenue source since 2007, succeeding because they limit to one relevant sponsor per week
Gruber built his own display ad system as a homegrown replacement after The Deck ad network shut down
The Talk Show podcast sponsorships are sold through Neat.fm with first-time sponsor discounts
Repeat sponsors returning for subsequent weeks serves as the key proof that the advertising model works
money_quotes: There's a part of me that loathes posting self-promotional stuff here on Daring Fireball. There's another part of me that wants to sell ads and keep this thing afloat.
They've succeeded, I think, because they make everyone happy.

There's only one sponsor per week and the sponsors are always relevant to at least some sizable portion of the DF audience, so you, the reader, are never annoyed and hopefully often intrigued by them.
My favorite thing about them is how many sponsors return for subsequent weeks after seeing the results.
key_insight: Daring Fireball's advertising model works because it constrains supply to one sponsor per week, ensuring relevance to readers while delivering results that bring sponsors back repeatedly.
